name: github-analyzer
description: Analyzes private and public GitHub repositories using a Personal Access Token. Requires a GitHub PAT stored in the vault (canonical path: 'github/pat'). For public-only repos without secrets, use github-analyzer-public (http-node) instead.
setup_instructions: 1. Create a GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) at https://github.com/settings/tokens with repo scope
2. Store it at the canonical vault path: create_secret {name: 'github/pat', value: 'ghp_...', namespace: 'integrations'}
3. Install with the vault path: install_module_from_catalog {name: 'github-analyzer', allowed_secrets: ['github/pat']}
4. In node config, set secret_name = 'github/pat' and repository = 'owner/repo'
Note: The legacy canonical path 'github_token' is also supported — use whichever path you stored the secret under, and pass the same path in both allowed_secrets and secret_name config.

search_pattern.title: Search Regex
search_pattern.description: Optional regular expression to search for in commit messages (e.g., '(?i)(fix|bug|issue)')

secret_name.title: Secret Name
secret_name.description: Vault path for the GitHub PAT. Canonical path: 'github/pat'. Must match the path used when the secret was stored and when the module was installed with allowed_secrets.
